channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Mind-bending photos by anonymous cousins show the pain and dreams of Afghan women
item.description: The young women make photos that look at life — how it is, how they wish it could be — under Taliban rule. The images are on display at the Photoville Festival in Brooklyn, New York.
item.content:encoded:
The young women make photos that look at life — how it is, how they wish it could be — under Taliban rule. The images are on display at the Photoville Festival in Brooklyn, New York.

(Image credit: Mahnaz Ebrahimi|January 2026)
item.dc:creator: Diane Cole

item.title: A Mississippi warehouse full of comic books is at the center of a legal battle
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Bloomberg reporter Jonathan Randles about a legal battle that's left over 8 million comic books sitting in a Mississippi warehouse.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to Bloomberg reporter Jonathan Randles about a legal battle that's left over 8 million comic books sitting in a Mississippi warehouse.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Visual impairment doesn't keep these birders out of the hobby
item.description: The phrase "bird watching" does not take in the full range of people who love searching for wild birds. We meet a few of the many visually impaired birders who use their ears.
item.content:encoded: The phrase "bird watching" does not take in the full range of people who love searching for wild birds. We meet a few of the many visually impaired birders who use their ears.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Eve Cohen

item.title: 15 movies we can't wait to see this summer, from Spielberg to 'Spider-Man'
item.description: The Toy Story squad takes on a tablet, the Minions take on Hollywood and Christopher Nolan takes on a Greek epic.
item.content:encoded: The Toy Story squad takes on a tablet, the Minions take on Hollywood and Christopher Nolan takes on a Greek epic.
item.dc:creator: Bob Mondello

item.title: A kick takes on a life of its own in the kids' book, 'When Tad Kicked Vlad'
item.description: On his birthday, Tad's best friend Vlad eats the very last slice of cake. Tad is mad so Tad kicks Vlad, kicking off a chain of kicks that travels around the world.
item.content:encoded: On his birthday, Tad's best friend Vlad eats the very last slice of cake. Tad is mad so Tad kicks Vlad, kicking off a chain of kicks that travels around the world.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Balaban

item.title: Romanian director Cristian Mungiu's 'Fjord' wins top prize at Cannes
item.description: Mungiu took home the prestigious Palme D'Or for his film Fjord, a culture-war drama set in Norway.
item.content:encoded:
Fjord — and Sebastian Stan, during the awards ceremony at the 79th Cannes international film festival, in southern France, on Saturday.'/>

Mungiu took home the prestigious Palme D'Or for his film Fjord, a culture-war drama set in Norway.

(Image credit: Andreea Alexandru)
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for May 23, 2026: Saying farewell to Bill Kurtis with Brandi Carlile!
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, special guest Brandi Carlile and panelists Luke Burbank, Negin Farsad, and Paula Poundstone
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, special guest Brandi Carlile and panelists Luke Burbank, Negin Farsad, and Paula Poundstone

(Image credit: Kevin C. Cox)

item.title: Paul Clayton discusses his role in corporate acting as AI characters gain popularity
item.description: In some workplace training videos, actors are being replaced by AI. NPR's Scott Simon talks to actor Paul Clayton, who has appeared in more than 1,000 corporate acting roles.
item.content:encoded: In some workplace training videos, actors are being replaced by AI. NPR's Scott Simon talks to actor Paul Clayton, who has appeared in more than 1,000 corporate acting roles.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: A film festival bridges two communities at the U.S.-Mexico border
item.description: Audiences on both sides of the U.S.-Mexico border wall in Nogales, Arizona and Nogales, Sonora gathered to watch the same films at the same time at a cross-border film festival.
item.content:encoded: Audiences on both sides of the U.S.-Mexico border wall in Nogales, Arizona and Nogales, Sonora gathered to watch the same films at the same time at a cross-border film festival.
item.dc:creator: Nina Kravinsky

item.title: Shein buys Everlane, which sold millennials the dream of ethical, affordable luxury
item.description: Everlane's finances have faltered in recent years. But will the merger alienate Everlane's existing shoppers — or sway droves of Shein fans to trade up?
item.content:encoded:
Everlane's finances have faltered in recent years. But will the merger alienate Everlane's existing shoppers — or sway droves of Shein fans to trade up?

(Image credit: Smith Collection/Gado/Getty Images
)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh
